items.text: DiskStore implements the NodeStore trait with on-disk content-addressed storage.
Node file path is determined by BLAKE3 hash: first 2 hex bytes as directory, remaining as filename.
Nodes are compressed with zstd before writing to disk (ADR-006).
Reads decompress zstd on load; the LRU cache stores decompressed nodes.
Node writes are idempotent: writing a hash that already exists on disk is a no-op.
LRU cache has a configurable maximum size in entries or bytes.
Cache eviction does not affect correctness — a miss falls through to disk read (CN6).
DiskStore exposes a delete-by-hash method for snapshot pruning coordination.
Each shard actor's WAL buffer is backed by an append-only file on disk.
WAL entries include operation type, key, value (for puts), and a CRC32 checksum (CN2).
Mutations are written to the durable WAL before the shard actor acknowledges the write.
Fsync policy is configurable: per-write, every N writes, or commit-only.
On commit (tree flush), the WAL file is truncated with a truncation marker recording the committed root hash.
WAL truncation is atomic: truncation marker and root hash are written together (CN5).
Recovery reads the last truncation marker to find the committed root hash.
Recovery verifies the committed root hash exists in the disk node store.
Recovery replays all WAL entries after the truncation marker, reconstructing the in-memory buffer.
Corrupted WAL entries (CRC mismatch) are detected and recovery replays up to the last valid entry.
Recovery is per-shard: each shard recovers independently (CN3).
After recovery, the shard actor resumes normal operation accepting new writes.
